steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content:
Each term in the expression has a factor of $6x$:
$$
6x^9-6x^8+72x
$$

steps.title: Divide each term
steps.content:
Divide every term by $6x$ to see what remains inside the parentheses:
$$
\frac{6x^9}{6x}=x^8
$$
$$
\frac{-6x^8}{6x}=-x^7
$$
$$
\frac{72x}{6x}=12
$$

steps.title: Write the factored form
steps.content: Putting those terms inside parentheses gives the equivalent factored form $$ 6x(x^8-x^7+12) $$ This keeps every original term unchanged when distributed. The answer is D.
